title: Chilaquiles Red Salsa

seo.title: Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe: Authentic Mexican Breakfast
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make the best Chilaquiles Red Salsa recipe at home! This authentic Mexican breakfast dish is easy, flavorful, and perfect for any morning. Get the full recipe now!

Chilaquiles Red Salsa step by step
best Chilaquiles Red Salsa at home
seo.h1: Authentic Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe: Your New Favorite Mexican Breakfast
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Chilaquiles Red Salsa
Serving Suggestions for Your Mexican Breakfast
seo.imageAlt: A plate of vibrant Chilaquiles Red Salsa with fried eggs, crema, and cilantro, ready for a delicious breakfast.
seo.imageCaption: Start your day with a burst of flavor! This homemade Chilaquiles Red Salsa recipe is a true taste of Mexico.

description: Chilaquiles Red Salsa is a beloved Mexican breakfast featuring crispy fried tortilla pieces bathed in a rich, spicy red salsa. Topped with fresh cheese, crema, and often a fried egg, it's a hearty and flavorful dish that truly wakes up your taste buds. This traditional recipe brings the authentic flavors of Mexico right to your kitchen table.

ingredients.notes: cut into triangles, or 8 oz store-bought tortilla chips
for frying tortillas, plus 2 tbsp for salsa
dried, stems removed, seeds optional for less heat
roughly chopped, plus 1/4 cup finely diced for garnish

instructions.title: Prepare the Tortilla Chips
instructions.text: If using fresh corn tortillas, heat 1/2 cup of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry the tortilla triangles in batches until golden and crispy, about 2-3 minutes per batch. Remove with a slotted spoon and place on a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil. Season lightly with salt. If using store-bought chips, skip this step.

instructions.title: Roast Chiles and Tomatoes
instructions.text: Place the halved Roma tomatoes, dried chiles (Arbol and Guajillo, stems and seeds removed from Guajillo), and 1/2 white onion onto a dry comal or skillet over medium heat. Roast them, turning occasionally, until the tomatoes are softened and slightly charred, the chiles are fragrant (about 1-2 minutes per side for chiles, be careful not to burn), and the onion is translucent. Remove the chiles immediately once fragrant to prevent bitterness.

instructions.title: Rehydrate Chiles and Blend Salsa
instructions.text: Place the roasted chiles in a small bowl and cover them with hot water for 5 minutes to rehydrate. Drain. Transfer the rehydrated chiles, roasted tomatoes, roasted onion, and garlic cloves to a blender. Add 1 cup of chicken broth and a pinch of salt. Blend until completely smooth. If the salsa is too thick, add a little more broth or water.

instructions.title: Cook the Red Salsa
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a clean large skillet or pot over medium heat. Carefully pour the blended salsa through a fine-mesh sieve into the hot oil, pressing down on the solids to extract all liquid. Discard the solids. Bring the salsa to a simmer and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it has thickened slightly and deepened in color. Season with additional salt to taste.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer Chilaquiles
instructions.text: Add the prepared tortilla chips to the simmering red salsa. Gently toss the chips to coat them evenly with the sauce. Cook for 2-3 minutes, just until the chips are softened but still retain some texture. Do not overcook, or they will become too soggy.

instructions.title: Serve Your Authentic Chilaquiles
instructions.text: Immediately transfer the Chilaquiles Red Salsa to individual plates. Garnish generously with crumbled queso fresco, a drizzle of Mexican crema, and a sprinkle of fresh chopped cilantro. For an even heartier meal, top each serving with a freshly fried egg. Serve hot and enjoy this classic Mexican breakfast!

tips: For a deeper flavor, char the tomatoes, onion, and garlic more intensely before blending. This adds a smoky complexity to your salsa.
Adjust the spice level by controlling the amount of chile de árbol. For less heat, remove the seeds; for more, add a few extra chiles.
Serve immediately after combining chips and salsa to prevent the chips from becoming too soggy. The perfect Chilaquiles have a slight bite.
If you prefer a thicker salsa, you can cook it for a bit longer. For a thinner sauce, add a splash more broth or water during blending.
Don't skip the straining step for the salsa; it ensures a smooth, restaurant-quality texture without any chile skin bits.
article:
Wake up your taste buds with a vibrant explosion of flavor! If you’re searching for a breakfast dish that’s both comforting and exhilarating, look no further than Chilaquiles Red Salsa. This classic Mexican breakfast is a culinary masterpiece, transforming humble fried corn tortillas into a saucy, cheesy, and utterly satisfying meal. Forget your usual scrambled eggs; chilaquiles offer a rich tapestry of textures and tastes, making them a beloved staple in Mexican homes and a must-try for anyone exploring authentic cuisine. Whether you’re a seasoned chef or a home cook looking for an exciting new challenge, mastering the art of Chilaquiles Red Salsa will undoubtedly impress and delight.

## What is Chilaquiles Red Salsa?

Chilaquiles (pronounced chee-lah-KEE-lehs) is a traditional Mexican dish that typically consists of lightly fried corn tortillas cut into quarters, simmered in a savory salsa until softened but still holding some texture. While green salsa (salsa verde) chilaquiles are also very popular, red salsa chilaquiles offer a deeper, often spicier, and more robust flavor profile, thanks to the use of ripe tomatoes and various dried or fresh red chilies. The dish is almost always topped with crumbled queso fresco or cotija cheese, a drizzle of Mexican crema (or sour cream), and fresh onion and cilantro. Often, it's served with a fried egg on top, shredded chicken, or a side of refried beans, making it a hearty and complete breakfast or brunch option. It’s the ultimate comfort food, perfect for weekend mornings or even as a late-night snack.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Chilaquiles

Creating the perfect Chilaquiles Red Salsa requires a few key components. Fresh, high-quality ingredients make all the difference.

For the Red Salsa:
*   6-8 ripe Roma tomatoes, roughly chopped
*   2-3 serrano or jalapeño peppers (adjust to your spice preference), stemmed
*   1/2 white onion, roughly chopped
*   2 cloves garlic, peeled
*   2 cups chicken broth or vegetable broth
*   1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped
*   1 tablespoon vegetable oil
*   Salt to taste

For the Tortillas:
*   12-15 corn tortillas (preferably a day old, or slightly dried)
*   Vegetable oil for frying (about 2-3 cups)

For Toppings:
*   1/2 cup crumbled queso fresco or cotija cheese
*   1/4 cup Mexican crema or sour cream
*   1/4 white onion, finely diced
*   1/4 cup fresh cilantro, chopped
*   Optional: Fried eggs, shredded cooked chicken, sliced avocado

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Red Chilaquiles

Follow this Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe to create an authentic and delicious Mexican breakfast.

1.  **Prepare the Salsa:** In a medium pot, combine the chopped tomatoes, serrano/jalapeño peppers, and the half onion. Cover with water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer for about 10-15 minutes, or until the vegetables are very soft. Drain the water, reserving about 1/2 cup. Transfer the cooked vegetables to a blender along with the peeled garlic, 1 cup of chicken broth, and 1/4 cup of fresh cilantro. Blend until smooth. If the salsa is too thick, add more reserved cooking water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency (it should be thick enough to coat a spoon). Season with salt to taste.
2.  **Cook the Salsa:** Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large saucepan or Dutch oven over medium heat. Pour the blended salsa into the hot oil. Be careful as it might splatter. Bring the salsa to a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally, and cook for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the salsa to thicken slightly. Keep it warm.
3.  **Prepare the Tortilla Chips:** While the salsa is simmering, prepare your tortillas. Stack the tortillas and cut them into quarters or eighths, forming triangles. Heat 2-3 cups of vegetable oil in a large, heavy-bottomed skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat until it reaches about 350-375°F (175-190°C). Fry the tortilla pieces in batches, without overcrowding the pan, until golden brown and crispy. This usually takes 2-3 minutes per batch. Using a slotted spoon, transfer the fried tortillas to a plate lined with paper towels to drain excess oil. Sprinkle lightly with salt.
4.  **Assemble the Chilaquiles:** Once all the tortillas are fried, add them directly to the simmering red salsa. Stir gently to coat all the tortilla pieces evenly. Cook for 2-4 minutes, or until the tortillas have softened slightly but still retain some chewiness. You don't want them to become completely mushy.
5.  **Serve Immediately:** Ladle the Chilaquiles Red Salsa onto individual plates. Garnish generously with crumbled queso fresco or cotija cheese, a drizzle of Mexican crema, finely diced white onion, and fresh cilantro. If desired, top with a perfectly fried egg or a serving of shredded chicken.

## Pro Tips for the Best Chilaquiles Red Salsa

Achieving chilaquiles perfection involves a few insider tricks. Firstly, don't skimp on the quality of your chilies and tomatoes; they form the backbone of your red salsa. Roasting your tomatoes, onions, and chilies before blending can add a deeper, smoky flavor profile to your salsa, a step often preferred over boiling. When frying your tortillas, ensure the oil is hot enough to crisp them quickly without absorbing too much oil. Also, the trick to perfectly textured chilaquiles lies in the timing: add the tortillas to the salsa just before serving. This allows them to soften slightly while retaining a delightful bite, preventing them from becoming soggy. For a more authentic kick, consider adding a dried chili like chile de árbol or guajillo (rehydrated and blended) to your Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Mexican Breakfast

Chilaquiles Red Salsa is a meal in itself, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Mexican breakfast accompaniments. A classic serving often includes a perfectly fried or scrambled egg perched atop the saucy tortillas, adding a rich, runny yolk that mingles beautifully with the salsa. For a more substantial meal, consider adding shredded chicken, thinly sliced carne asada, or even a chorizo crumble. A side of creamy refried beans or seasoned black beans complements the dish wonderfully, as does a slice of fresh avocado or a dollop of guacamole. To complete your Mexican breakfast experience, serve with a steaming cup of café de olla or a refreshing glass of agua fresca.

## Variations and Substitutions for Chilaquiles

While the traditional Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe is a classic for a reason, the beauty of this dish lies in its versatility. For those who prefer a milder flavor, reduce the number of serrano or jalapeño peppers, or use milder chilies like poblano. If you’re short on time, you can use good quality store-bought tortilla chips (thick cut is best) instead of frying your own, though fresh-fried offers superior texture. For a vegetarian or vegan version, simply use vegetable broth instead of chicken broth, and opt for vegan cheese and crema alternatives. You can also experiment with different types of chilies in your salsa; dried ancho or guajillo chilies, rehydrated and blended, create a rich, smoky red salsa. Some recipes even call for a touch of epazote or cumin for an added layer of flavor.

Chilaquiles Red Salsa is more than just a breakfast; it's a celebration of Mexican culinary heritage. Its layers of flavor, satisfying textures, and vibrant appearance make it an unforgettable dish. Whether you're making it for a special brunch or a comforting weeknight meal, this Chilaquiles Red Salsa Recipe promises a delicious journey into the heart of Mexican cuisine. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to fall in love with this incredible dish.
